overview: A schedule management prototype for Surface Experts. Mobile-first day view for techs/sales, desktop multi-column view for ops. Mock JSON data, drag-and-drop, conflict detection, dark chrome industrial design.

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding and Configuration
userStories.description: Initialize React + Vite project with Tailwind CSS, Google Fonts (Bebas Neue, Open Sans), ESLint, and folder structure. Configure base styles matching the dark chrome design system.
acceptanceCriteria: Vite project created with React template
Tailwind CSS installed and configured with custom colors (charcoal: #1A1A1A, accent: #F47A20, etc.)
Google Fonts loaded: Bebas Neue for headings, Open Sans for body
ESLint configured with React rules
Folder structure: src/components/, src/data/, src/utils/, src/hooks/, src/layouts/
index.css sets dark background (#1A1A1A) and default font (Open Sans)
App.jsx renders a placeholder with correct fonts and colors visible
npm run dev starts without errors
npm run lint passes

userStories.title: Mock Data Files and Data Access Layer
userStories.description: Create all JSON mock data files (team members, events, event types) and a centralized data access utility. All components will import from this single access layer.
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/teamMembers.json with 5-6 team members (3 techs, 2 sales, 1 ops)
src/data/events.json with 25-30 events across the current week following role-based distribution
src/data/eventTypes.json with all 6 event types and their colors
src/utils/dataAccess.js exports: getAllMembers(), getMemberById(id), getEventsForMember(memberId, date), getEventsForDate(date), getEventTypes(), getEventTypeByKey(key)
Events use realistic titles (apartment names, unit numbers for jobs; company names for sales stops)
Mix of job statuses: ~60% open, ~25% closed-no-invoice, ~15% closed-invoiced
src/data/README.md documenting all schemas

userStories.title: App Layout Shell and Routing
userStories.description: Create the responsive app shell with mobile top bar and desktop sidebar. Set up React Router with a main schedule route.
acceptanceCriteria: React Router configured with / route for schedule view
Desktop sidebar: fixed left, dark background (#1A1A1A), logo placeholder at top, nav links in middle, user avatar/name at bottom, search bar with dark fill and rounded shape
Mobile top bar: dark background, hamburger icon on right, logo on left, current month/year in center
Hamburger menu opens a slide-out drawer with same nav as desktop sidebar
Responsive breakpoint: sidebar shows at md (768px+), top bar shows below md
Active nav item highlighted with orange accent
Sidebar and top bar use Bebas Neue for headings, Open Sans for labels

userStories.title: Mobile Week Strip Navigation
userStories.description: Build the week strip component that shows 7 day squares for the current week with navigation arrows to cycle between weeks. Current day selected by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Seven squares in a row, each showing abbreviated day name on top and numeric date on bottom
Left and right arrows on either side to navigate to previous/next week
Current day selected by default with orange background and white text
Tapping a day selects it and deselects the previous one
Selected date is passed up to parent via callback
Component is mobile-only (hidden on desktop)
Uses date-fns for date calculations, week starts on Monday

userStories.title: Mobile Day Agenda Calendar Grid
userStories.description: Build the day agenda view for mobile that displays time slots from 6 AM to 8 PM with 15-minute increments. Empty grid only, no events rendered yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Vertical time grid from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM
Hour labels on the left side (6 AM, 7 AM, ... 8 PM)
15-minute grid lines (lighter for quarter hours, heavier for full hours)
Grid takes up remaining screen height below the week strip
Grid is scrollable vertically
Current time indicator: horizontal orange line at current time position
Grid cell height is consistent and proportional (each 15-min slot = same height)

userStories.title: Event Card Component
userStories.description: Create the event card component that displays within time grid slots. Cards are colored by event type and show status indicators for jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Card renders with white background, colored left border (4px, color from event type)
Card height is proportional to event duration (15 min = 1 slot height)
Card shows: event title, start-end time, event type label
Job-type events show status indicator dot in top-right corner
Status indicators: no dot for open, yellow dot (#EAB308) for closed-no-invoice, purple dot (#8B5CF6) for closed-invoiced
Card text truncates with ellipsis if card is too small
Cards for events shorter than 30 minutes show condensed layout (title only)

userStories.title: Render Events on Mobile Calendar
userStories.description: Integrate event cards into the mobile day agenda grid. Events for the selected person and selected date render as positioned blocks on the time grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Events render at correct vertical position based on start time
Events span correct height based on duration
Events positioned in the right column area (to the right of time labels)
Changing the selected date from week strip updates displayed events
Default view shows current user's events for today
Empty state: helpful message when no events exist for selected day/person

userStories.title: Mobile FAB - Create Event Actions
userStories.description: Build the floating action button (bottom-right) that expands to show event creation options, following the Google Calendar FAB pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Orange circular FAB (56px) in bottom-right corner with plus icon
Pressing FAB: fades/dims background, reveals action options above the FAB
Action options: one button per event type with colored indicators
Pressing an action opens the create event modal with that type pre-selected
Pressing dimmed background or X button closes the FAB menu
FAB has subtle scale animation on open/close

userStories.title: Mobile FAB - Team Member Switcher
userStories.description: Build the bottom-left floating action button that shows a list of team members to switch whose schedule is displayed.
acceptanceCriteria: Circular button (48px) in bottom-left showing current user's initials
Pressing button shows vertical list of team members above the button
Each option shows avatar/initials and name
Current user always appears at the bottom of the list
Selecting a team member switches the calendar to that person's schedule
Currently selected member highlighted with orange accent
Pressing backdrop closes the member list

userStories.title: Create Event Modal - Mobile
userStories.description: Build the bottom-sheet modal for creating new events on mobile. Slides up from the bottom of the screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal slides up from bottom with drag handle at top
Fields: Event Type dropdown, Person dropdown (defaults to viewed person), Date picker (defaults to selected date), Start Time (15-min increments), End Time (15-min increments), Title text input
Event Type dropdown shows colored indicators next to each option
Time pickers only allow 15-minute increments from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM
End time must be after start time - inline validation error if not
Save button (orange pill) and Cancel button (dark pill)
Saving adds event to local state and closes modal

userStories.title: Edit Event Modal - Mobile
userStories.description: Build the bottom-sheet modal for editing existing events. Same layout as create modal but pre-populated and includes delete option.
acceptanceCriteria: Opens when tapping an existing event card
Same slide-up bottom sheet as create modal
All fields pre-populated with event's current values
Same validation rules as create modal
Save button updates the event in local state
Delete button (red text) with confirmation prompt removes the event
Cancel closes without changes

userStories.title: Long-Press to Create Event on Mobile
userStories.description: Implement long-press on an empty time slot to auto-create an event at that time and open the create modal.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-pressing (500ms hold) on empty time slot opens create event modal
Start time set to the slot that was pressed
End time defaults to start time + 1 hour
Person defaults to currently viewed team member
Visual feedback during long press (subtle highlight on slot)
Long-pressing event card opens edit modal instead

userStories.title: Mobile Drag-and-Drop - Move Events
userStories.description: Implement drag-and-drop to move events to different time slots on the mobile day view.
acceptanceCriteria: Touch-drag an event card to move it to a different time slot
Event snaps to nearest 15-minute increment on drop
Visual ghost/preview shows where event will land while dragging
Invalid drop targets (conflicting times) visually indicated
Dropping on conflicting slot shows toast error and reverts
Event data updates in state on successful drop

userStories.title: Mobile Drag-to-Resize Events
userStories.description: Allow dragging the bottom edge of an event card to extend or shorten its duration.
acceptanceCriteria: Resize handle visible at bottom of each event card
Dragging handle down extends event's end time
Dragging handle up shortens event's end time
Minimum duration is 15 minutes
End time snaps to 15-minute increments
Resizing into a conflicting event is blocked with visual feedback and toast

userStories.title: Desktop Date Picker Bar
userStories.description: Build the top date picker bar for desktop that shows the current date and allows day-by-day navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontal bar at top of main content area (right of sidebar)
Shows current selected date prominently in Bebas Neue
Left/right arrows for previous/next day navigation
Today button to jump back to current date
Accepts external date updates (for scroll-sync)
Desktop only (hidden on mobile)

userStories.title: Desktop Multi-Column Time Grid
userStories.description: Build the desktop calendar grid that shows one day at a time with each team member as a column.
acceptanceCriteria: Column header row showing each team member's name and avatar/initials
One column per team member
Time labels (6 AM - 8 PM) on far left shared across all columns
15-minute grid lines within each column
Grid is vertically scrollable
Columns evenly distributed across available width
Current time indicator line spans across all columns

userStories.title: Render Events on Desktop Grid
userStories.description: Render event cards within the desktop multi-column grid, positioned correctly in each team member's column.
acceptanceCriteria: Events render in the correct team member's column
Events positioned at correct vertical time offset
Event cards use same EventCard component as mobile
All team member events for selected date visible simultaneously
Changing date updates all columns

userStories.title: Desktop Continuous Day Scroll with Sticky Headers
userStories.description: Enable scrolling past end of one day into the next on desktop. Sticky day header between days and date picker auto-updates on scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: After 8 PM of current day, grid continues with day separator header for next day
Day separator shows date in Bebas Neue and is sticky at top of viewport while scrolling
Scrolling into a new day updates the DesktopDatePicker
Navigating via date picker scrolls grid to that day's section
At least 5 days rendered below selected day for smooth scrolling

userStories.title: Desktop Click-to-Create Event
userStories.description: Clicking an empty time slot in any team member's column opens the create event modal with that time and person pre-selected.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking empty slot opens centered modal for creating event
Clicked time pre-set as start time, end time defaults to +1 hour
Team member whose column was clicked pre-set as person
Modal uses same form fields as mobile create modal
Modal appears centered on screen with backdrop overlay
Save adds event, Cancel closes modal

userStories.title: Desktop Edit Event Modal
userStories.description: Clicking an existing event on desktop opens a centered edit modal with pre-populated data and delete option.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking event card on desktop opens centered edit modal
All fields pre-populated with event data
Same validation as mobile edit modal
Save updates event, Delete removes with confirmation
Cancel closes without changes

userStories.title: Desktop Drag-and-Drop Within Column
userStories.description: Enable drag-and-drop of events within the same team member's column to change event time.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag event card up/down within column to change time
Event snaps to nearest 15-minute increment on drop
Ghost/preview shows target position while dragging
Conflict detection: invalid targets highlighted, drop blocked with toast
Event time updates in state on successful drop

userStories.title: Desktop Drag-and-Drop Between Columns
userStories.description: Enable dragging events between team member columns on desktop to reassign events.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag event from one column to another to reassign it
Dropping in different column changes event's assigneeId
Time can change simultaneously with reassignment
Conflict detection against target person's existing events
Invalid targets shown with visual feedback, drop blocked with toast on conflict
Event data (assignee and time) updates in state on successful drop

userStories.title: Polish and Responsive Transitions
userStories.description: Final polish pass - smooth transitions between mobile and desktop, consistent styling, loading states, and overall visual quality.
acceptanceCriteria: Resizing browser between mobile and desktop transitions cleanly
All text uses correct fonts (Bebas Neue headings, Open Sans body)
All buttons match pill style from design system
Orange accent consistent across active states, borders, primary buttons
No visible UI bugs at 375px, 768px, and 1280px viewports
Toast notifications styled to match design system
All interactive elements have hover/active brightness shift states
